prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sampled commercial begins with a completely black frame, with no visible objects, people, or text.

By the final sampled frame, the scene has changed to a beach setting. Several children appear as dark silhouettes running or playing along the shoreline, with ocean waves in the background. Large black text is superimposed over the upper and middle part of the image. The visible text reads in part: “Just made for boys:” and “... shirts.” Lower lines include partially readable wording such as “Of Ban- ... Caprolan nylon” and “by Allied Chemical.” Some of the central text is obscured by a dark rectangular shape or graphic, making it unclear.

No other events between the opening black frame and the final beach/text image are visible in the sampled frames.
